title: Milestone 2: CLI App
subtitle: Loops and Logic in action.
goal: Build a command-line app using loops, arrays, and logic.

blocks.heading: Building a Task Manager
blocks.html: Now that you know loops and methods, you can build a real app that runs continuously. We're going to build a To-Do List app.

blocks.heading: The Requirements
blocks.html: Open IntelliJ. Create Todo.java. The program should loop forever until the user types '3' (Quit). It should offer choices: 1. View tasks, 2. Add a task, or 3. Quit.

blocks.caption: Starter structure

blocks.heading: Hints & Tips
blocks.html:
• Array limits: Remember arrays have a fixed size. Use taskCount to know where to insert the next task (tasks[taskCount] = "new task"; taskCount++;).
  • Scanner trap: If you use scanner.nextLine() after nextInt(), it might consume the enter key. Use an extra scanner.nextLine() to clear it!

blocks.heading: Push it up
blocks.html: Don't forget to push this new project to GitHub! Create a new repository for it.

blocks.title: Match concepts
blocks.instructions: Click a card on the left, then its match on the right!

pairs.right: Reads user input from the keyboard
Repeats code as long as a condition is true
Chooses between two paths based on a condition
Prints a line to the console

challenge.title: Complete your To-Do App
challenge.instructions: Build the app on your computer and push it to GitHub.
challenge.solution: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) {} }
challenge.passMsg: 🏆 You've built a persistent CLI app! Great job.
